name: Zaytun Msabbah (Spiced Olives)
recipeInstructions:
1. Either leave the olives as they are or cut 3 to 4 slits in each with a fine-bladed, stainless steel knife or razor blade. Discard any damaged olives.
2. Pack the olives into sterilized glass jars and cover with cold water. Soak for 3 days, changing the water each day.
3. Measure the last amount of water used after soaking.
4. In a pan, measure the same amount of fresh water and add rock salt in the proportion of 1/3 cup to each 4 cups of water. Heat and stir until the salt dissolves. Allow the solution to cool.
5. Pack the chilies into the jars, placing them among the olives. Pour the cooled brine over the top, filling the jars. Remove any air bubbles and seal with a plastic lid.
6. Store in a cool, dark place for 5 months before using.
7. To serve, remove the desired amount of olives and rinse under cold water. Drain and place in a bowl. Squeeze the juice of a lemon over the olives and pour on 1/4 cup olive oil. Stir to blend and allow to sit for an hour or two before serving.
